qna_pairs.Gold Answer: Fresh spinach should be cleaned thoroughly and then can be stored loosely in an unsealed bag in the crisper tray of the refrigerator for a few days. For longer storage, it should be fresh frozen, cooked and frozen or canned. Storage in the freezer can be for up to eight months.

qna_pairs.Query: What happens to the nutritional value of spinach after storage
qna_pairs.Gold Answer: Spinach loses much of its nutritional value with storage of more than a few days. While refrigeration slows this effect to about eight days, spinach will lose most of its folate and carotenoid content.

Plain cooked spinach is best served steamed or boiled in a minimum amount of water for no more than five minutes. The bulk of the leaves reduces enormously in cooking. Allow about 100 g per serving and use the biggest pan you have. Serve with lemon juice, or perhaps with salt.
